name: Akkala Sami

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Akkala Saami (akka1237).

notes: Skeleton. Akkala Sami is a nearly extinct Kola (Eastern) Saami language of the Uralic family, very close to Kildin Sami and with only a marginal written tradition; where written it uses a Cyrillic alphabet modelled on the Kildin conventions, which this map follows. Base Cyrillic letters are mapped, together with Ӈ (velar nasal), the voiceless lateral Ӆ and Һ (preaspiration). Palatalization, preaspiration and vowel length are not recoverable from the spelling and are left unencoded; because the orthographic norm itself is unsettled, a high PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Akkala Saami (akka1237)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
